LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED SECURITY BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO INVASTION OF PRIVACY CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE DISCLOSURE OF THE PAYEE'S IDENTITY WAS NECESSARY TO CLEAR THE CHECK. THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION ON INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS DENIED BECAUSE UNITED SECURITY BANK IS NOT A DEFENDANT ON THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CERTAIN CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH THE PLAINTIFFS DID NOT OPPOSE. THESE ARE THE CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO WRONGFUL TERMINATION, VIOLATION OF THE RIGHT OF FREE ASSOCIATION, LABOR CODE VIOLATION AS AGAINST UNITED SECURITY BANK. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE FILED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF 2 DECLARATIONS IN SUPPORT OF REPLY IS DENIED. THE COURT ALSO RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS FILED BY THE DEFENDANTS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID DOUGLAS DOYLE, ESQ. of Doyle & Schallert, Tel No. 559-227-2600 and atty. TYLER H. LESTER, ESQ., Tel No. 559-432-5977 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Attys. TRACEY K. MERWISE, ESQ. and CONOR J. DALE, ESQ. of Jackson Lewis P.C., Tel No. 415-394-9400 appearing for Defendant MUFG Union Bank, N.A. f/k/a Union Bank, N.A.;
Atty. TIMOTHY L. THOMPSON, ESQ. of McCormick, Barstow, Sheppard, Wayte & Carruth LLP, Tel No. 559-433-1300 appearing for Defendant United Security Bank.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MUFG UNION BANK, N.A. F/K/A UNION BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The court grants summary adjudication as to the wrongful termination claim. The undisputed evidence shows that Braly, an employee of United Security Bank, was the person who unnecessarily disclosed the private information. (UMF 72). Defendant was merely the recipient of the information. There was no public policy violation. D's anti-loan policy is reasonable. It is a way to avoid conflicts of interest and instill confidence in the banking industry, which is a highly regulated. See Barbee v. Household Automotive Finance Corp. (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 525, 536. The motion is granted as to the 2nd cause of action for violation of Labor 96(k). There is no private right of action under that statute. Id. The motion is granted as to the 3rd cause of action for PAGA violation because there was no underlying violation of the Labor Code. See Arias v. Sup. Crt. (2009) 46 Cal.4th 969, 987. The motion is granted as to the 4th cause of action for invasion of privacy. Ms. Braly was the person that invaded the privacy interest by disclosing the information to defendant. The motion is granted as to the declaratory relief cause of action. Declaratory relief acts prospectively and there is no basis for injunctive relief because defendant's practice does not violate public policy. The motion is denied as to causes of action 8 and 9. Rebolledo's dec. at par. 21-25 sufficiently establishes the amount of uncompensated work. See Hernandez v. Mendoza (1988) 199 Cal.App.3d 721, 727. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEFENDANT UNITED SECURITY BANK''''S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION of the sixth cause of action is GRANTED. Defendant''s evidence shifts the burden by establishing Braly was unaware that Alvarado was a United Bank employee, that United Bank prohibited loans between employees, or that the check was a loan between Rebolledo and Alvarado. Plaintiffs fail to present substantial, non-speculative evidence to dispute these facts; without such knowledge, United Securities Bank cannot be liable for this cause of action because there is no proof of knowledge or an intentional attempt to interfere with Plaintiffs'''''''' employment contracts with United Bank. RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA REBOLLEDO AND HEATHER ALVARADO'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiffs may file their proposed First Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this Order. "If the motion to amend is timely made and the granting of the motion will not prejudice the opposing party, it is error to refuse permission to amend and where the refusal also results in a party being deprived of the right to assert a meritorious cause of action or a meritorious defense, it is not only error but an abuse of discretion." (Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Plaintiffs timely moved to amend the complaint. The prejudice asserted by the Defendant in opposition to this motion can be alleviated by continuing the trial date. The January 12, 2015 trial date is continued to September 14, 2015 per the agreement of the parties. 2 ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Mark Schallert, Esq. of Doyle & Schallert, Tel. 415.994.6537 and Tyler Lester, Esq. of Tyler H. Lester, PC, Tel. 559.432.5977, for plaintiffs and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT UNITED SECURITY BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to invastion of privacy cause of action. Defendants have failed to demonstrate that the disclosure of the payee's identity was necessary to clear the check. The 4th cause of action on injunctive relief is denied because United Security Bank is not a defendant on that cause of action. Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to certain causes of action which the plaintiffs did not oppose. These are the causes of action as to wrongful termination, violation of the right of free association, labor code violation as against United Security Bank. Defendants' request for judicial notice filed with the moving papers is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice of 2 declarations in support of reply is denied. The Court also ruled on the evidentiary objections filed by the defendants. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
